rulings.Case Title: JOHN H. CARDOZA et al VS. 3M COMPANY, INDIVIDUALLY AND AS PARENT, ALTER EGO, et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, December 2, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 15. Defendant Union Carbide Corporation¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Plaintiff failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether the Paco joint compound which Mrs. Cardoza was exposed to contained asbestos supplied by defendant. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM FATHERREE et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, December 2, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Defendant Elliott Company¿s motion for summary judgment is off calendar at the moving party¿s request. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM TORCHIA VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, December 2, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 5. Defendant Contra Costa Electric, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant did not satisfy its burden of production that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials disturbed by defendant. Plaintiff¿s deposition testimony and written discovery responses preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES HETZEL VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, December 2, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 6. Defendant FDCC California, Inc.¿s (fka Dinwiddie Construction Co.) motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant satisfied its burden of production and plaintiff fid not present admissible evidence that he was exposed to asbestos attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s objections to the deposition testimony of Mr. Verhalen and Mr. Johnson and the declaration of Mr. Ay are sustained. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: CARL MCCURTIS VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al

rulings.Case Title: JOHN MCFADDEN VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, December 2, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 8. Defendant Albay Construction Co.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant did not satisfy its burden of production that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials disturbed by defendant. Plaintiff¿s deposition testimony precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Even if defendant did satisfy its burden of production, plaintiff created a triable fact on this issue with his declaration and deposition testimony and the declaration of Mr. Cohen. = (220/HEK)
